Functions for drawing speed lines in different contexts for timeline animations.

# Purpose
This C header file defines functions and a macro related to drawing speed lines in a graphical application. The macro `PEEK_ANIMATIONS_SPEED_LINES_OFFSET_X` is set to a value of 7, which likely represents a horizontal offset for the speed lines. The file declares three functions: [`peek_animations_draw_compositor_foreground_speed_lines`](<#peek_animations_draw_compositor_foreground_speed_lines>), [`peek_animations_draw_compositor_background_speed_lines`](<#peek_animations_draw_compositor_background_speed_lines>), and [`peek_animations_draw_timeline_speed_lines`](<#peek_animations_draw_timeline_speed_lines>). Each function takes a `GContext` pointer and a `GPoint` structure as parameters, which are used to specify the drawing context and the offset position for rendering the speed lines. These functions are intended for use in rendering different layers or components of a graphical user interface.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `applib/graphics/gtypes.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### peek\_animations\_draw\_compositor\_foreground\_speed\_lines<!-- {{#callable_declaration:peek_animations_draw_compositor_foreground_speed_lines}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/popups/timeline/peek_animations.h#L23>)

Draws foreground speed lines for animations on a graphics context.
- **Description**: Use this function to render foreground speed lines on a given graphics context, which can be part of an animation sequence. It requires a valid graphics context and an offset point to determine where the drawing begins. The function modifies the graphics context by drawing vertical lines at specified positions and heights, creating a visual effect of speed lines. Ensure that the graphics context is properly initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `ctx`: A pointer to a `GContext` object where the speed lines will be drawn. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership.
    - `offset`: A `GPoint` structure that specifies the starting offset for drawing the speed lines. The function uses this offset to position the lines on the graphics context.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`peek_animations_draw_compositor_foreground_speed_lines`](<peek_animations.c.md#peek_animations_draw_compositor_foreground_speed_lines>)  (Implementation)

### peek\_animations\_draw\_compositor\_background\_speed\_lines<!-- {{#callable_declaration:peek_animations_draw_compositor_background_speed_lines}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/popups/timeline/peek_animations.h#L25>)

Draws background speed lines for a compositor animation.
- **Description**: Use this function to render background speed lines in a compositor animation context. It requires a valid graphics context and an offset point to determine the position of the lines. This function is typically used in graphical applications where dynamic background effects are needed. Ensure that the graphics context is properly initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `ctx`: A pointer to a `GContext` object representing the graphics context. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership.
    - `offset`: A `GPoint` structure specifying the offset for drawing the speed lines. The function uses this to position the lines correctly.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`peek_animations_draw_compositor_background_speed_lines`](<peek_animations.c.md#peek_animations_draw_compositor_background_speed_lines>)  (Implementation)
